title: rubus × rhodanensis
intro: Rubus × rhodanensis, also known as the French raspberry or Alpine raspberry, is a hybrid species of the Rubus genus that combines the characteristics of the wild raspberry (Rubus idaeus) and the black raspberry (Rubus occidentalis). The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, primarily due to its high anthocyanin and ellagic acid content. The health benefits of Rubus × rhodanensis include its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and certain types of cancer. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is often used in traditional medicine to support digestive health and boost the immune system.
